payslip.howTitle: Found a gap? Here's how to act
howSteps: Contact the employer in writing (email to payroll / your manager) and ask for an explanation and correction — usually a mistake fixed at once.
Keep a copy of the request and the relevant payslips.
If unresolved — you can approach the Labor Rights Commissioner at the Labor Ministry, or consult an employment lawyer. Wage claims are possible up to 7 years back.
Missing pension — also verify with the pension fund that deposits actually arrived.

trustPage.title: Security & Trust
trustPage.intro: Zakai is built around trust: we act on your behalf toward companies and handle sensitive financial data. This page explains, transparently, how we protect your data and how the fee model works.
security: Encryption in transit: all communication with the site is encrypted over HTTPS/TLS, with an HSTS policy that enforces a secure connection.
Encryption at rest: data is stored in a managed database with storage-layer encryption.
Secrets & keys: API keys and secrets are never in the code — they are injected only as secure environment variables.
Verification codes: the SMS ownership code is stored hashed only (never in the clear), with expiry and an attempt cap.
Rate limiting: login, signup and code-sending endpoints are rate-limited to prevent abuse.
Monitoring: system errors are logged and monitored so issues are detected and fixed quickly.
Dependency scanning: software dependencies are scanned automatically for known vulnerabilities.
trust: We charge an 18% fee only from documented actual savings (a documented difference between the original and new amount). No saving — no charge.
Every request is sent only after your explicit approval, and after account-ownership verification plus an authorization document with a code the company can check.
Every request discloses to the company that Zakai is a digital agent acting on your behalf — we never impersonate you.
No overblown promises: Zakai is an automated tool that drafts and sends requests, not an 'expert' with human experience.
trustPage.dispute: When a fee is charged, we email you an automatic confirmation with a full breakdown (original amount, new amount, saving and fee). If you believe the saving did not actually materialize, you have {days} days from receiving the email to contact us for review — and if the saving turns out not to have taken effect, the fee is cancelled or refunded. Contact: {email}
trustPage.contact: Found a security issue or have a question? We'd like to hear from you: {email}
trustPage.disclaimer: This document describes the prototype's security and trust policy. A commercial version will be accompanied by full legal documents (Terms of Use and Privacy Policy) drafted by a lawyer. Nothing here is legal advice.

flights.title: Flight cancelled or delayed? You're owed money.
flights.subtitle: Israel's Aviation Services Law obligates airlines to fixed compensation, an alternative ticket and assistance — low-cost carriers included. Three taps to know what you're owed.
flights.noticeQ: When were you notified of the cancellation?
flights.noticeShort: Less than 14 days before the flight
flights.resultTitle: What the law entitles you to
flights.refund: Ticket refund or an alternative flight — your choice
flights.assistance: Airport assistance: food and drink, communication, and lodging when needed
flights.notEntitled: In this case the law grants no automatic entitlement. If you suffered real damages, other grounds may exist — keep your receipts.

notes.noticeExempt: With 14+ days notice there is no automatic monetary compensation, but you are owed a refund or an alternative ticket.
notes.shortDelay: A delay under two hours grants no entitlement under the law.
notes.longDelayAsCancellation: A delay of more than 8 hours counts as a cancelled flight under the law.
notes.exceptions: Airlines have narrow statutory exemptions (e.g. extraordinary circumstances beyond their control). Compensation is not automatic in those cases.
notes.indexed: Statutory amounts (2023 base) are index-linked — the exact figure is set at claim time.
notes.eu3h: Under EU case law, a 3h+ arrival delay is compensated like a cancellation.
notes.euExceptions: Airlines are exempt in 'extraordinary circumstances' (extreme weather, certain strikes); ordinary technical faults usually are NOT exempt.

flights.disclaimer: Based on the Aviation Services Law (Compensation and Assistance for Flight Cancellation or Change of Conditions), 2012. Not legal advice. Coming soon: Zakai prepares the full demand for you — ready for you to send yourself.
flights.euDisclaimer: Based on Regulation (EC) 261/2004 — flights departing the EU, or arriving in the EU on an EU carrier. Not legal advice. Coming soon: Zakai prepares the full demand for you — ready for you to send yourself.

terms.p: Zakai is a consumer self-help digital service: it checks bills and entitlements, builds comparisons and generates outreach letters — and for consumer matters (such as a mobile or electricity bill) it may relay a request to the provider as the user's messenger, with explicit per-request authorization. Zakai is not a lawyer, tax adviser, pension adviser, insurance agent or investment adviser; provides no legal or professional advice of any kind; does not represent the user in any legal or quasi-judicial proceeding; and does not perform acts reserved by law to lawyers. Demand letters and legal checks are self-help templates — the user reviews, adopts and sends them, in their own name and on their own responsibility.

All estimates and recommendations are orientation only. Zakai does not guarantee savings, compensation or any outcome; actual results depend on factors beyond our control. Calculations are based on the data the user enters — wrong, partial or outdated input may produce a wrong result, and responsibility for the accuracy of the data rests solely with the user. Results are not a substitute for checking with the competent body (the provider, the authority, an accountant or a lawyer) or for professional advice.

A success fee is charged only on savings documented in the system, at the rate of the user's plan at documentation time. Users have 14 days to dispute a charge; if the saving did not materialize, the fee is cancelled or refunded.

A subscription (Pro/Max) is a continuing transaction, cancellable at any time from the settings screen with no penalty; cancellation takes effect from the next cycle and does not affect what was already provided. Under the Consumer Protection Law, 1981, a distance transaction carries a 14-day cancellation right from receipt of the service or the disclosure document, whichever is later. To cancel: the settings screen or the support address.

Relaying a request to a provider in consumer matters is done as a messenger only — in the user's name, after ownership verification and explicit authorization limited to that request. The authorization is not a power of attorney for legal representation, does not empower Zakai to conduct proceedings or bind the user, and is revocable at any time — from which point no new outreach is made.
Provide accurate information, use the service only for accounts you own, and make no unlawful, abusive or infringing use.
Zakai may suspend or terminate accounts for breach, abuse or suspected fraud, with notice where possible. Users may delete their account at any time from Settings.

The service is provided AS IS. To the maximum extent permitted by law, Zakai's liability is capped at the fees actually paid by the user in the 12 months preceding the event. Nothing here limits liability that cannot be limited by law.
All rights in the service, code, design and content belong to Zakai.
We may update these terms; material changes will be surfaced in the product. Continued use constitutes acceptance.
Israeli law governs; exclusive jurisdiction lies with the competent courts in Israel.

privacy.p: Account details (name, email, phone), information you upload for checks (bills, amounts), and records of actions taken on your behalf. Providing it is not a legal obligation — but the service cannot run without it.

No third-party tracking, advertising or analytics cookies. One essential session cookie only. The public tools — subscription scan, electricity calculator, flight checker, rights check and the Zakameter — run entirely in your browser; their inputs are never sent or stored.

Operating the service only: bill analysis, authorized outreach, savings documentation and fee calculation, security and fraud prevention, and legal compliance. We do not sell personal data or share it for marketing.

Only as required to operate: with the provider being contacted on your behalf (minimum necessary), secured infrastructure vendors (hosting, messaging, server-side AI), or a competent authority under law.
Access, correction and deletion. Account deletion — one click in Settings — permanently erases all personal data, checks, authorizations and history.
Encrypted transport (HTTPS/HSTS), one-way password hashing, hashed expiring rate-limited verification codes, restricted and logged access. Material incidents are reported as required by law.
Data is kept while the account is active or as required by law; deletion erases personal data permanently.
Consent to the terms and this policy is recorded at signup and shown in Settings. Material changes will be surfaced in the product.
Privacy questions and rights requests: support@zakai.example (to be updated). We respond within the statutory timeframe.

miluim.title: Served in the reserves? Let's verify you were paid right.
miluim.subtitle: Hundreds of thousands have served since October 2023 — and most calculations miss the 20% supplement almost nobody knows. Estimate in seconds; the math runs in your browser only.
miluim.salaryQ: Average monthly gross (3 months before service)
miluim.incomeQ: Average monthly insured income (3 months before)
miluim.howTitle: How to actually get the money
howSteps: Employee: your employer must pay your regular salary during service and claim reimbursement (form BL/501).
Check your payslip covers the service period in full — including the supplement. A gap = money your employer owes you.
Not paid within ~3 weeks of the service's end? File a personal claim (form BL/502) on the National Insurance site.
Self-employed: paid directly to you — verify it arrived and used insured income + 25%.
Keep your service confirmations (3010) — they anchor every claim.
miluim.disclaimer: Estimate per National Insurance rules (3-month basis ÷ 90 + 20% supplement). Index-linked daily minimums/maximums may adjust the figure; the NII calculation governs. Not advice. Coming soon: Zakai prepares the forms for you.

severance.title: Severance calculator
severance.subtitle: An employee dismissed after a year is entitled to one month's salary per year of work. Enter your last salary and tenure — Zakai computes what you're owed. Runs in your browser, no documents.
severance.notEligible: Under one year — severance entitlement usually begins after a full year of work (barring exceptions).
severance.howTitle: How to make sure you got it all
howSteps: Under a Section 14 arrangement, much of the severance is already accrued in your pension/managers-insurance and released to you even on resignation. Check with the fund.
Compare what you received (release letter / final payslip) to the figure here.
If short — ask the employer in writing to correct and complete it.
Unresolved? Approach the Labor Rights Commissioner or an employment lawyer. Severance claims are possible up to 7 years back.
severance.disclaimer: The calculation is based on the Severance Pay Law (one month's salary per year) and the data you enter. The exact base and entitlement depend on your agreement, qualifying pay components and circumstances of termination. Not legal advice; a conservative estimate.

maternity.title: Maternity allowance calculator
maternity.subtitle: Bituach Leumi pays maternity allowance based on your pre-birth salary. Enter your monthly salary and qualifying period — Zakai computes what you're owed. Runs in your browser, no documents.
maternity.salaryQ: Average monthly salary (3 months before birth)
maternity.capped: Salary exceeded the ceiling — the allowance is limited to the maximum (₪1,730.33/day, 2026).
maternity.howTitle: How to make sure it's right
howSteps: Maternity allowance is paid automatically after birth — check in your Bituach Leumi account that the amount matches your salary.
If you worked for several employers — make sure all income was counted.
The determining wage is the 3 months before stopping work; if those were unusually low, alternative rules apply — worth checking with a representative.
Missing or wrong? File a written objection with Bituach Leumi.
maternity.disclaimer: The calculation is based on Bituach Leumi's maternity rules (salary/30 per day, up to the ceiling) and the data you enter. The exact entitlement and number of days depend on your qualifying period and circumstances. Not advice; a conservative estimate.

taxrefund.title: Tax-refund estimator
taxrefund.subtitle: The most common reason for a refund: you worked only part of the year. Tax was withheld as if you'd earn all year — but you earned less, so a refund is due. A quick estimate, in your browser.
taxrefund.resultSub: An estimate using the 2026 brackets and credit-point value. The exact amount is set when you file with the Tax Authority.
taxrefund.noRefund: No refund from this reason given your input — but you may have one from other reasons (pension deposits, donations, a job change). Worth checking.
howSteps: Open a personal area on the Tax Authority site and file a refund request (form 135) — checking and filing are free.
You can file up to 6 years back — each year separately.
Attach the 106 forms from every employer that year.
Other refund reasons: pension/study-fund deposits, donations to recognized institutions, unused credit points (degree, immigrant, discharged soldier, children).
taxrefund.disclaimer: The estimate is based on the 2026 brackets and credit-point value and the data you enter, and covers only the 'worked part of the year' reason. It excludes other reasons, other income and personal deductions, and is not tax advice. The binding amount is set by the Tax Authority.

check.title: Quick check: do you likely have a tax refund waiting?
check.sub: Tick anything true for you in the last 6 years. Each item is a common reason for a refund.
triggers: Worked only part of the year (started/left mid-year, or a student)
Changed jobs or worked several jobs at once
Had a period of unemployment, unpaid leave or maternity leave
Was discharged from military/national service in the last 6 years
Donated to a recognized institution (Section 46 credit)
Made independent pension or study-fund deposits
You or a family member has a recognized disability
Withdrew money from a provident or severance fund
check.none: Nothing ticked — you might still be owed. Worth checking the calculator below.
check.hit: Good sign! {n} of these apply to you — you likely have a refund waiting. You can file up to 6 years back.

unemployment.title: Unemployment benefit estimator
unemployment.subtitle: Dismissed or finished a job? You may be owed unemployment benefit from Bituach Leumi. Enter salary and age for an estimate — how much per day and for how many days. Runs in your browser.
unemployment.perDay: per day (range by the benefit tiers)
unemployment.capped: High salary — the benefit is limited to the cap (₪550.76/day for the first 125 days, 2026).
howSteps: Register at the Employment Service immediately after leaving work, and check in as required.
Open an unemployment claim on the Bituach Leumi site (needs 12 of the prior 18 months worked).
Attach the employment-termination form (BL/1500) from your employer.
For an exact figure — the official unemployment calculator on the Bituach Leumi site.
unemployment.disclaimer: This is an estimate only: the benefit is computed on a regressive scale (80/50/45/30 percent of wage) up to a cap, and depends on age, dependents and exact wage. Shown as a range on purpose. Not advice; the binding amount is set by Bituach Leumi.

parking.title: Parking-ticket appeal
parking.subtitle: Many parking tickets fall on defects — unclear signage, a broken meter, wrong details. Fill a few fields and Zakai prepares an objection letter for you to send in your own name. Runs in your browser.
parking.name: Full name

signage.label: Unclear signage
signage.body: There was no clear, legible sign prohibiting parking at the location, or the sign was hidden/damaged — so it was not possible to know parking was forbidden.

machine.label: Broken payment meter
machine.body: I paid or attempted to pay for parking, but the meter / app did not work properly at the time — so the ticket is not justified.

loading.label: Loading/unloading
loading.body: I stopped briefly only for loading and unloading, which is permitted in these circumstances, not for extended parking.

disabled.label: Disabled / permit
disabled.body: I hold a valid disabled tag / parking permit that was displayed as required, so parking was permitted.

details.label: Wrong details on ticket
details.body: The ticket contains incorrect details (vehicle number / location / time), a defect that justifies cancelling it.

other.label: Other
other.body: I dispute the justification of the ticket in the circumstances, as detailed below.

parking.sendHint: Send it to the local authority within 90 days of receiving the ticket (email / online form / post), and keep a copy.
parking.legal: Zakai is not a lawyer and does not represent you. This is a self-help tool: the letter is a template you review, adopt and send in your own name and on your own responsibility. If the appeal is refused, you have the right to a hearing at the local-affairs court.
parking.disclaimer: The letter is generated in your browser only and is never sent or stored. Not legal advice; appeal grounds and deadlines vary by authority and circumstances.

lostMoney.metaTitle: Lost money — dormant pensions & insurance | Zakai
lostMoney.metaDesc: Billions of shekels sit in lost pension, insurance and savings accounts in Israel. Here is how to check if some of it is yours — with the free official tools.
lostMoney.title: You may have lost money — and not even know it
lostMoney.sub: Billions of shekels sit in forgotten pension funds, insurance policies, provident funds and bank accounts across Israel. Some of it could be yours or your family's. Here is exactly where to check — with the state's free official tools.

sources.title: Har HaKesef
sources.body: The Finance Ministry's official search for pension accounts, provident funds, study funds and managers' insurance in your name — including ones forgotten from old employers.

sources.title: Har HaBituach
sources.body: Find every active insurance policy in your name — life, health, long-term care. People often discover they pay twice for the same cover.

sources.title: Dormant bank accounts
sources.body: The Bank of Israel runs a search for dormant accounts and deposits — money that sat untouched for years and can be withdrawn.

sources.title: National Insurance rights
sources.body: Sometimes benefits or grants go unclaimed. Check with National Insurance what is open in your name.

steps: Have your ID ready and log in with government identification — all official tools require identity verification.
Search each tool — Har HaKesef, Har HaBituach and dormant accounts — for yourself and, with their consent, for family members.
Found an account? Contact the body that manages it (insurer, investment house or bank) to withdraw or consolidate.
Consider consolidating scattered pension funds — lower fees, more money that stays with you.

cta.title: Want to know what else you are owed?
cta.body: Beyond lost money, there are dozens of rights and refunds you can claim. A short quiz shows what is relevant to you.

lostMoney.disclaimer: Zakai points you to the state's free official tools and does not charge for it. We have no access to your accounts, and this is not pension or financial advice — the search and withdrawal are done by you with the official bodies.

baggage.metaTitle: Delayed or lost baggage? You're owed compensation | Zakai
baggage.metaDesc: Under the Montreal Convention you may be owed up to ~$2,175 for baggage delay or loss. Most travelers never claim it. Here's how to claim right.
baggage.title: Baggage delayed or lost? You're owed far more than you think
baggage.sub: Almost every international flight is covered by the Montreal Convention, which entitles you to compensation for expenses caused by baggage delay or loss. Airlines offer a small voucher — but you're owed much more.
baggage.capNote: (1,519 SDR) per passenger — for essential expenses caused by the delay or loss. It's a cap, not a fixed sum; you claim actual expenses.
rights: Reimbursement for essential purchases (clothes, toiletries) you had to make while your bag was delayed — up to the cap.
Total loss of a bag (usually after 21 days) entitles you to compensation for the full contents, up to the cap.
A refund of baggage fees you paid if the service wasn't properly provided.
The right stands on its own — even if the airline offered you a voucher or small 'goodwill' payment.
steps: At the airport immediately: file a Property Irregularity Report (PIR) at the airline desk. Without that number claiming is hard.
Keep every receipt for essential purchases made because of the delay — photograph them.
Submit a written compensation claim to the airline with the PIR and receipts, within the Convention's window (21 days for delay).
Don't sign a 'full and final' settlement for a low sum before checking what you're owed on actual expenses.
traps: They present an internal '~$50/day guideline' as if it's the legal cap — it isn't. The cap is far higher.
They treat a reimbursement as an 'advance' and deduct it from the final payout — insist it's separate.
They demand original receipts only and reject photos — keep both the original and a digital copy.

cta.title: Flew recently? Check compensation for the flight delay too
cta.body: Beyond baggage, a delay or cancellation of the flight itself can entitle you to hundreds of euros more under EC261 and Israel's Aviation Services Law.

baggage.disclaimer: Zakai is a self-help tool, not legal advice. Compensation depends on circumstances, route and carrier policy, and is not guaranteed. The Montreal Convention applies to most international flights; check your flight details.

bankFees.metaTitle: Bank fees & late charges — how to get them waived | Zakai
bankFees.metaDesc: Households pay hundreds a year in bank fees that can be reduced or waived. Here's how to ask, with a ready-to-send letter.
bankFees.title: You're paying bank fees you could simply cancel
bankFees.sub: Transaction fees, account fees, overdraft charges and late-payment interest add up to hundreds a year. Most banks have a waiver policy on request — but only if you ask. Here's how, with a ready letter.

fees.name: Teller / transaction fees
fees.note: You can usually move to a cheaper fixed fee plan, or waive fees on actions you can do yourself in the app.

fees.name: Account / securities management fees
fees.note: Negotiable, especially for a long-standing customer or one considering switching banks.

fees.name: Overdraft / late-payment interest
fees.note: A one-time goodwill waiver is often granted if you have a clean payment history.

fees.name: FX conversion & card-abroad fees
fees.note: Compare no-conversion-fee plans/cards — the gap adds up on every trip.

steps: Download your recent months' fee breakdown (in the bank's site/app) and mark the anomalies.
Write to the bank's customer service or via the app — in writing it's documented and easier to escalate.
Show a clean payment history and ask for a cheaper fee plan + a waiver of the anomalous charges.
No answer? You can escalate to the Bank of Israel ombudsman — just mentioning it usually speeds a reply.
bankFees.templateSub: Copy it, fill in the details in brackets, and send it yourself to your bank. This is a letter you send in your own name — Zakai does not represent you.
bankFees.template:
To Customer Service / Public Inquiries, [Bank name],

My name is [full name], account holder no. [account number] at branch [branch number].

Reviewing my fee breakdown for recent months I found the following charges I'd like reconsidered: [list the fees and dates]. I am a long-standing customer with a clean payment history.

I request: (1) to waive the overdraft/late fees listed as part of customer-retention policy; (2) to move to the most cost-effective fee plan for me; (3) a written breakdown of management fees and reduction options.

I'd appreciate a written reply within a reasonable time. Thank you,
[full name] · [phone] · [date]

cta.title: Want us to find more needless charges?
cta.body: A statement scan spots duplicate subscriptions, forgotten recurring charges and anomalous fees — in one place.

bankFees.disclaimer: Zakai is a self-help tool, not financial advice. A waiver is at the bank's discretion and subject to your account terms; no outcome is guaranteed. The letter is sent by you, in your name.

priceProtection.metaTitle: Price dropped after you bought? You may be owed the difference | Zakai
priceProtection.metaDesc: Hotels, flights and online orders drop in price after booking. In many cases you're owed a refund of the difference. Here's how to check and claim.
priceProtection.title: Price dropped after you bought? You're often owed the difference
priceProtection.sub: Hotel, flight and product prices change daily. If the price dropped a few days after you booked — in many cases you're owed a refund, a cheaper rebook-and-cancel, or a credit. Most people simply never check.

wheres.title: Hotels (free cancellation)
wheres.body: In ~30%–40% of flexible-cancellation bookings the price drops at least once before arrival. Just rebook at the new price and cancel the old one.

wheres.title: Flights
wheres.body: Some airlines credit the difference or a voucher if the price drops within a window after purchase — check the carrier's price policy.

wheres.title: Online shopping
wheres.body: Many sites (and some credit cards) offer 'price protection' and refund the difference if the item drops within 7–14 days of purchase.

steps: Keep the booking confirmation (email) with the price and date — that's your reference point.
Re-check the price of the same room/flight/product in the following days (especially 7–14 days).
Price dropped? If there's free cancellation — rebook cheaper and cancel the expensive one. Otherwise ask the seller for a difference refund under their price-protection policy.
Not sure? Screenshot the new price (with date) and send a written credit request to customer service.

cta.title: Want more moves that save you money right away?
cta.body: We gathered real money-saving moves in one place — mobile, electricity, bank, rights and more.

priceProtection.disclaimer: Zakai is a self-help tool. The right to a difference refund depends on the seller's/issuer's policy and the deal terms, and is not guaranteed. The check and the request are done by you.

warranty.metaTitle: Product warranty — claim a repair or replacement | Zakai
warranty.metaDesc: Device broke within warranty? You're owed a repair, replacement or refund. Here's how to claim, even without the receipt, with a ready letter.
warranty.kicker: A right you already paid for
warranty.title: Device broke within warranty? Don't pay for the repair
warranty.sub: By law, many products come with a one-to-three-year warranty. People give up because they lost the receipt or the importer stalls. But your right stands — here's how to claim it, with a ready letter.
rights: Free repair of a defect-related fault within the warranty period — no parts or labor cost.
Not fixed after a reasonable number of attempts? You're owed a replacement or refund.
The importer/manufacturer is responsible for the repair even if you bought from a store that closed — the warranty travels with them.
Some credit cards auto-extend warranty — check if you paid with one and whether extended cover applies.
steps: Find proof of purchase: a receipt, an email invoice, or the card charge from that date — any of them is valid.
Locate the device's serial number and document the fault (photo/video).
Write to the importer or manufacturer demanding a repair/replacement under law, with the proof attached.
No answer? You can escalate to the Consumer Protection Authority — the inquiry itself usually speeds a reply.
warranty.templateSub: Copy it, fill in the details in brackets, and send it yourself to the importer/manufacturer. This is a letter you send in your own name — Zakai does not represent you.
warranty.template:
To Customer Service, [importer/manufacturer name],

My name is [full name]. On [purchase date] I bought [product name and model], serial number [serial], as shown in the attached proof of purchase.

The product is within its warranty period and recently developed a fault: [describe the fault]. I request to exercise the warranty and repair the defect at no cost, and if it cannot be repaired in a reasonable number of attempts — to replace it or refund me, as provided by law.

I'd be glad to schedule a time and receive a written reply within a reasonable period. Thank you,
[full name] · [phone] · [date]

cta.title: Want to see what other rights you're owed?
cta.body: Zakai's rights engine shows relevant rights for your situation out of dozens — tax, national insurance, consumer and more.

warranty.disclaimer: Zakai is a self-help tool, not legal advice. A warranty claim depends on the warranty terms, the type of fault and applicable law; no outcome is guaranteed. The request is sent by you, in your name.

arnona.metaTitle: Arnona appeal — refund an over-charge | Zakai
arnona.metaDesc: Roughly 1 in 3 properties is over-charged arnona — wrong measurement, wrong classification, or a missed discount. Here's how to check and file an objection, with a ready letter.
arnona.title: You may be over-paying arnona — and you can get it back
arnona.sub: Arnona (municipal property tax) is one of a household's largest fixed costs, and measurement and classification errors are very common. One correction is worth hundreds to thousands of shekels a year — every year. Here's exactly what to check and how to file an objection.
arnona.errorsTitle: Where it usually goes against you

errors.name: Over-measured area
errors.note: The city charges for a larger area than the real one — the most common and most worthwhile error to check.

errors.name: Wrong classification
errors.note: Residential charged as business, or a service area (storage/balcony) charged as primary area at a higher rate.

errors.name: Missed discount
errors.note: Senior, single parent, disability or low income — discounts you're owed that weren't applied automatically.

errors.name: Wrong billing zone
errors.note: Each zone in the city has a different rate; a wrong assignment inflates the charge for nothing.

arnona.stepsTitle: How to file an objection — step by step
steps: Ask the municipality for the property's measurement and classification data (freedom-of-information request or via the authority's site).
Compare the area to the plan/actual measurement, and check whether a discount you're owed wasn't applied.
File a written objection with the Arnona Manager — usually within 90 days of the annual charge notice.
Denied? You have the right to appeal to the arnona objections committee, and then to the administrative court.
arnona.templateSub: Fill in your details, tick the relevant reasons, and send it yourself to the municipality's Arnona Manager.
arnona.template:
To
The Arnona Manager, ____ Municipality

Re: Objection to arnona (municipal property tax) charge — property at ____, payer no. ____

My name is ____, the occupier of the property above. Under the Local Authorities (Objection to General Arnona) Law, 1976, I hereby object to the arnona charge, on the following grounds:

[ ] The charged area is incorrect / over-measured versus the actual area.
[ ] The property classification is wrong (e.g. residential charged as business).
[ ] Discounts/exemptions I am entitled to were not applied (senior / single parent / disability / low income).
[ ] The billing zone does not match the property's location.

Please provide the measurement and classification data the charge is based on, correct the charge accordingly, and credit me retroactively for any overcharge.

I would appreciate a reasoned written reply within the statutory period. Should the objection be denied, I reserve my right to appeal to the arnona objections committee.

Respectfully,
____
Date: ____

cta.title: Want to check what else you're owed?
cta.body: Arnona is just one right. A short quiz shows other discounts and refunds you might be missing.

arnona.disclaimer: Zakai is a self-help tool, not a lawyer. The letter is a template you send in your own name, and is not legal advice. Objection outcomes depend on the property data and the municipality's policy.

dupInsurance.metaDesc: Israelis pay ~3.5B ILS a year on duplicate insurance. With indemnity policies you pay twice but can claim once. Here's how to spot duplicates and get a refund, with a ready letter.
dupInsurance.title: You may be paying for duplicate insurance — and can only claim once
dupInsurance.sub: Israelis pay around 3.5 billion shekels a year on duplicate policies covering exactly the same risk. In many cases you can't even claim twice — so you pay double and receive once. Here's how to spot it and get a refund.
dupInsurance.keyTitle: The distinction that saves you money: indemnity vs compensation
dupInsurance.keyBody: With indemnity policies (surgery, health, medication) the law forbids receiving more than the actual cost — so two duplicate policies mean double payment, single payout. Compensation policies (critical illness, life) can pay from several companies. The wasted duplication concentrates in indemnity policies.

areas.name: Surgery cover
areas.note: Commonly duplicated against the HMO's supplementary insurance — you pay for the same cover twice.

areas.name: Medication outside the basket
areas.note: Mostly covered by supplementary HMO cover; a parallel private policy is often redundant.

areas.name: Long-term care
areas.note: A group HMO policy alongside private long-term-care cover — check there is no overlap.

areas.name: Personal accident
areas.note: Duplication between workplace cover and a separately bought private policy.

dupInsurance.stepsTitle: How to check and get a refund
steps: Log in to 'Har HaBituach' (harb.cma.gov.il) and pull the list of all active policies in your name.
Flag indemnity policies covering the same risk in more than one place — that's where the duplication is.
Ask the insurer to cancel the redundant cover and refund duplicated premiums (ready letter below).
Denied? Approach the company's public-enquiries officer, then the Capital Market, Insurance and Savings Authority.
dupInsurance.templateSub: Fill in your details and send it to the insurer. You can send it to each company where you hold overlapping cover.
dupInsurance.template:
To
Customer Service / Public Enquiries, ____ (insurer)

Re: Request to check duplicate insurance and refund premiums — policy no. ____

My name is ____, the insured under the policy above. I suspect I hold duplicate indemnity-type cover (e.g. surgery / health / medication / long-term care) covering the same risk in another policy with you and/or another company.

With indemnity-type policies, one cannot receive more than the actual loss, so paying duplicate premiums does not buy me additional cover. Under the Insurance Contract Law and the Capital Market Authority's rules on selling duplicate policies, I request:

1. A full list of all active cover in my name with you and its type (indemnity / compensation).
2. A review of whether duplicate cover exists, and cancellation of the redundant cover.
3. A refund of premiums charged in duplicate, where duplicate cover was sold improperly and without informed consent.

I would appreciate a reasoned written reply. I reserve my right to approach your public-enquiries officer and the Capital Market Authority.

Respectfully,
____
Date: ____

cta.title: Want to find more money you're owed?
cta.body: Duplicate insurance is just the start. Also check lost pension, insurance and bank accounts in your name.

dupInsurance.disclaimer: Zakai is a self-help tool, not an insurance agent or pension adviser. The letter is a template you send in your own name. Do not cancel cover before confirming you don't need it — sometimes duplication is worthwhile, and the check is your responsibility with the authorized parties.

deposit.metaTitle: Rental deposit refund — landlord won't return it? | Zakai
deposit.metaDesc: Landlord withholding your deposit? They may only deduct proven damage, not reasonable wear. Here's how to demand it back, with a ready letter.
deposit.title: Landlord not returning your deposit? You're owed it back
deposit.sub: A rental deposit secures your obligations — it's not the landlord's property. Only a proven debt may be deducted (unpaid rent, an open bill, real damage) — never reasonable wear or 'just because'. Here's how to get it back.
deposit.rulesTitle: What the landlord may deduct — and may not

rules.name: Reasonable wear — not deductible
rules.note: Ordinary use (faded paint, picture holes) is natural wear and can't be deducted from the deposit.

rules.name: Proven damage — only with evidence
rules.note: Real damage beyond wear can be deducted, but only against proof and a repair invoice.

rules.name: Open debts only
rules.note: Unpaid rent or bills (electricity/water/arnona) left open in your name — a legitimate deduction.

rules.name: Holding it 'as security' — unlawful
rules.note: The landlord can't keep the deposit as pressure or 'just in case' without a proven debt.

deposit.stepsTitle: How to demand your deposit back
steps: Document the apartment's condition on move-out (photos + meters) — your proof you left no damage.
Send the landlord a written demand to return the deposit within 14 days (ready letter below).
Demand a breakdown and evidence for any deduction — a deduction without proof isn't legitimate.
Not returned? You can file a small-claims case (up to the statutory cap) yourself, no lawyer needed.
deposit.templateSub: Fill in your details and send it to the landlord. Keep a copy and proof of delivery.
deposit.template:
To
____ (the landlord)

Re: Demand to return the rental deposit — apartment at ____

My name is ____. I rented the apartment above from ____ to ____. I vacated it and
returned the keys on ____. To date my deposit of ILS ____ has not been returned.

Under the Rental and Borrowing Law, 1971 (including the Fair Rental amendment),
the deposit secures my obligations only and is not your property. Only a
genuinely proven debt may be deducted (unpaid rent, open bills in my name, or
damage beyond reasonable wear) — and only against supporting evidence.

I therefore demand the full deposit of ILS ____ be returned within 14 days. If
you wish to deduct any amount, please provide a written breakdown and evidence
for each deduction. Absent a return or a reasoned reply, I will consider small-
claims proceedings to recover the sum plus costs.

Respectfully,
____
Date: ____

cta.body: A deposit is just one thing. Check in a few taps how much in total is worth checking for you.

deposit.disclaimer: Zakai is a self-help tool, not a lawyer. The letter is a template you send in your own name, and is not legal advice. The outcome depends on your contract terms and the circumstances.

childSavings.metaTitle: Savings for Every Child — don't leave your child's money on the floor | Zakai
childSavings.metaDesc: Every child in Israel has a state savings account. With the right track it can grow by thousands of shekels. Here's how to do it, free.
childSavings.title: Every child in Israel has a savings account — most lose thousands from it
childSavings.sub: Since 2015 the state deposits money every month into a 'Savings for Every Child' account for each child. Parents choose where it goes — but most leave it in the default track and miss thousands of shekels by age 18. Choosing is free and takes 5 minutes.
childSavings.bigNumberSub: Over 18 years, the gap between a conservative default track and an equity track can reach thousands of shekels — money that belongs to your child, without you adding a single shekel.

moves.title: Pick the right investment track
moves.body: By default the money sits in a bank at a low rate. Moving to a provident fund on an equity track (suited to an 18-year horizon) can multiply the return. Choosing is free on the National Insurance site.

moves.title: Double the deposit
moves.body: You can ask the state to double the monthly deposit from the child allowance — a matching sum joins in, at no extra cost to you. The money simply grows faster.

moves.title: Consolidate several kids' accounts
moves.body: Have several kids? Make sure they're all in the right body and track, watch the management fees, and don't forget the money when they turn 18.

steps: Log in to the National Insurance site and go to 'Savings for Every Child'.
Check which track the money is currently in (most likely: a conservative default).
Choose a provident fund on a long-horizon track, and consider asking to double the deposit from the allowance.
Repeat for each child, and set a reminder to review once a year.

cta.title: Don't leave your child's money on the floor
cta.body: Choosing is completely free and done by you on the official site. We just explain how to maximize it — because it's your child's money.
cta.primary: Go to Savings for Every Child
cta.secondary: What else is the family owed?

childSavings.disclaimer: Zakai explains and points to the state's free official tool and does not charge for it. This is not pension or investment advice — the choice is made by you on the National Insurance site, and past returns don't guarantee future ones.

constructionDefects.metaTitle: Construction defects — up to tens of thousands ₪ from the developer | Zakai
constructionDefects.metaDesc: Bought a new apartment? Under the Sale (Apartments) Law you have up to a 7-year defect-liability right. Most buyers never claim it. Here's how to check what you're owed.
constructionDefects.title: Bought a new apartment? You're owed compensation for every defect — up to 7 years back
constructionDefects.sub: The Sale (Apartments) Law requires the developer to fix construction defects, and to compensate you if they don't. The liability period runs up to 7 years depending on the defect. Most buyers give up because they don't know what they're owed — and the developer is happy. The initial check is free.
constructionDefects.bigNumberSub: An engineer's inspection of a new apartment typically finds dozens of defects; the repair/compensation value often reaches tens of thousands of shekels — money the developer owes you by law.

defects.title: Damp & sealing
defects.body: Water ingress, mold, poor sealing in wet rooms and balconies — among the most common and costly defects.

defects.title: Deviations & flooring
defects.body: Uneven floors, hollow or cracked tiles, wall deviations beyond the standard tolerance.

defects.title: Carpentry & aluminum
defects.body: Doors and windows that don't close properly, poor insulation, faulty hardware.

defects.title: Electrical & plumbing
defects.body: Missing/faulty electrical points, water pressure, drainage and sewage issues.

defects.title: Spec mismatch
defects.body: What you got differs from what the sale spec promised — inferior materials, dimensions or equipment.

defects.title: Insulation & acoustics
defects.body: Thermal/acoustic insulation below standard — cold in winter, hot in summer, and noisy.

constructionDefects.howTitle: How to claim it — the right way
steps: Don't sign 'apartment handover' without reservation if there are defects — document everything in writing.
Order an engineer's inspection (bdek bayit) — it quantifies the defects and the repair value. That's the document that speaks.
Send the developer a reasoned demand with the inspection report and a legal repair timeline.
Didn't fix it in reasonable time? You're owed monetary compensation (the repair cost) — usually on a success-fee basis, no upfront pay.

cta.title: Check what the developer owes you
cta.body: We start with a free initial check of your rights under the Sale (Apartments) Law. The inspection itself is done by a licensed engineer — we explain, guide and help you claim.

constructionDefects.disclaimer: Zakai is a self-help tool, not an engineer, appraiser or lawyer. Quantifying defects requires a licensed engineer's inspection, and compensation depends on their findings, the contract and the Sale Law. This is not engineering or legal advice and no outcome is promised.

classAction.metaTitle: Class-action settlements — money from settlements you don't know you're owed | Zakai
classAction.metaDesc: Every year dozens of class actions in Israel end in settlement — money or benefits for those affected. Most go unclaimed. Here's how to check if you're owed.
classAction.title: Used a product or service that was sued? You may be owed settlement money
classAction.sub: Every year dozens of class actions against companies (banks, telecom, insurance, retail) end in settlements — compensation or a benefit for everyone harmed. The problem: most consumers don't know they're part of the class, and never claim. The check is free.
classAction.bigNumberSub: Some settlements pay out automatically; others require a short claim. If you were a customer of the company in the relevant period — you may be owed, without doing anything.
classAction.examplesTitle: Common settlement types you might be part of

examples.title: Bank/credit fees
examples.body: Banks and credit companies sued over overcharged fees — the settlement refunds each affected customer.

examples.title: Telecom companies
examples.body: Charges for a service not provided, data, or plan terms — settlements refund subscribers.

examples.title: Retail & food
examples.body: Misleading labels, price or weight — compensation or benefit for anyone who bought in the relevant period.

examples.title: Insurance
examples.body: Overcharged premiums or unpaid claims — settlements refund affected policyholders.

classAction.howTitle: How to check if you're owed
steps: Check the official class-action registry — every claim and settlement in Israel is published there.
Search for companies you were a customer of (bank, telecom, insurance, chains) in the relevant period.
For a settlement that covers you — some are automatic (money just arrives), some need a short claim form by a deadline.
Don't miss the deadline — each settlement has a claim window. Keep proof of being a customer in the period.

cta.title: Check if a settlement owes you
cta.body: You can check the official class-action registry yourself — free. We explain how to spot a settlement that covers you and how to claim on time.

classAction.disclaimer: Zakai is a self-help tool, not a lawyer and does not represent you in proceedings. Eligibility depends on the specific settlement's terms, your membership in the class, and the deadlines set. This is not legal advice and no outcome is promised.

carValue.metaTitle: Car diminished value after an accident — compensation your insurer won't mention | Zakai
carValue.metaDesc: Were you in an accident? You're owed compensation for the car's diminished value — usually thousands of shekels. Insurers don't offer it. Here's how to claim.
carValue.title: Were you in an accident? You're owed diminished-value compensation nobody told you about
carValue.sub: Even after the car is repaired like new, its market value drops simply because it was in an accident — that's 'diminished value', and you're owed compensation for it. Insurers know this and don't offer it. Most drivers give up without knowing. The initial check is free.
carValue.bigNumber: Usually thousands ₪ — sometimes tens of thousands
carValue.bigNumberSub: Diminished value is calculated as a percentage of the car's value by severity and the parts damaged. On a newer/expensive car or a significant accident it can reach tens of thousands of shekels — money owed to you under the policy or from the at-fault party.

facts.title: It happens even after a perfect repair
facts.body: The mere accident record lowers the car's resale price. The compensation is for that gap — not the repair.

facts.title: The insurer won't offer it
facts.body: Insurers pay for the repair but 'forget' to mention diminished value. You have to demand it explicitly.

facts.title: Owed from the at-fault party too
facts.body: If a third party caused the accident, you're owed full diminished-value compensation from them/their insurer, on top of the repair.

facts.title: There's a time window
facts.body: A diminished-value claim has a limitation period. Best to act close to the event, while documentation is fresh.

carValue.howTitle: How to claim it — the right way
steps: Keep all documents: appraiser's report, repair invoice, garage confirmation and accident details.
Order an appraiser's diminished-value opinion — it quantifies how much the car's value dropped. That's the document that speaks.
Send a reasoned demand to the insurer (yours or the at-fault party's) with the appraisal.
Didn't pay what's owed? That's exactly a success-fee claim — no upfront pay.

cta.title: Check what you're owed for diminished value
cta.body: We start with a free initial check of your eligibility. The appraisal is done by a licensed appraiser — we explain, guide and help you claim.

carValue.disclaimer: Zakai is a self-help tool, not an appraiser, lawyer or insurer. Quantifying diminished value requires a licensed appraiser, and compensation depends on their findings, the policy and the accident circumstances. This is not professional advice and no outcome is promised.

mortgageInsurance.metaTitle: Overpriced mortgage insurance — you're paying the bank double | Zakai
mortgageInsurance.metaDesc: The bank sold you mortgage insurance (life + structure) marked up by tens of percent. Switching to an independent policy saves thousands ₪ a year — tens of thousands over the mortgage.
mortgageInsurance.title: The bank sells you overpriced mortgage insurance — you can save thousands a year
mortgageInsurance.sub: Every mortgage requires life and structure insurance. Banks sell it through themselves at a marked-up price — sometimes tens of percent above the open market. Switching to an identical independent policy saves thousands a year, and you lose nothing. The check is free.
mortgageInsurance.bigNumber: Thousands ₪ a year — tens of thousands over the mortgage
mortgageInsurance.bigNumberSub: The gap between the bank's insurance and an independent policy compounds over the 20-30 year mortgage into tens of thousands of shekels — money leaving your pocket for nothing.

facts.title: The bank doesn't have to be the insurer
facts.body: You must have insurance — but not from the bank. You may choose any insurer, and the bank must accept it.

facts.title: The gap is huge
facts.body: Insurance through the bank is usually tens of percent more expensive than an identical independent policy — for the exact same cover.

facts.title: You can switch mid-way too
facts.body: Not only when taking the mortgage — you can move to cheaper insurance at any stage, even years in.

facts.title: Same cover, less money
facts.body: Switching doesn't harm your cover or mortgage — the same protection, just cheaper.

steps: Gather your current mortgage insurance details (life + structure) and the monthly cost.
Compare against an identical independent policy on the open market — that's where the gap shows.
Switch to the cheaper insurance; the law requires the bank to accept a valid external policy.
Notify the bank of the switch — and the saving starts immediately, every month to the end of the mortgage.

cta.title: Check how much you're overpaying on mortgage insurance
cta.body: We start with a free initial check of the gap vs. the open market. We explain and guide; the switch itself is with a licensed insurer.

mortgageInsurance.disclaimer: Zakai is a self-help tool, not an insurance agent or advisor. Cover, price and eligibility depend on the insurer, the policy and your situation. This is not insurance advice and no outcome is promised.
